item.title: IMF About to Restore Venezuela Relations, Spain’s Cuerpo Says
item.description: Venezuela’s government is on track to regain formal relations with the International Monetary Fund this week, according to Spanish Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo.

item.title: UK’s Reeves Hints at Spending Cuts to Fund Defense Boost
item.description: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves signaled reluctance to raise taxes or borrow to pay for a boost to military spending as the government considers how to accelerate planned increases following the Iran war.

item.title: JPMorgan Released From OCC Order Over Trading Surveillance Gaps
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. was released from a two-year enforcement action by US regulators who had accused the biggest US bank of failing to properly monitor conduct of its employees and clients.

item.title: ECB’s Panetta Says Current Wars May Reverse Years of Development
item.description: Ongoing conflicts and global geopolitical tensions are threatening the future of the world’s developing countries, according to European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta.

item.title: Henry Paulson Says US Should Prepare for a ‘Vicious’ Bond Crash
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson called on US authorities to prepare a back-up plan in order to avert a potential collapse in demand for Treasuries — an event that he warned would have “vicious” effects.

item.title: Madison Air Up 16% After Biggest US Industrial IPO Since ‘99
item.description: Madison Air Solutions Corp. shares rose 16% after the company raised $2.23 billion in the biggest US listing of an industrial firm in close to three decades.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Doesn’t See Decisive Information on Iran Effects
item.description: Surveys the European Central Bank will receive ahead of its next policy meeting are unlikely to provide a clear picture of the fallout from the Iran war on Europe, Chief Economist Philip Lane said.

item.title: USMCA Annual Review Risks Halting Mexican Peso Rally, BBVA Says
item.description: Yearly reviews of the North American trade pact, a scenario that has become the base case to many in Mexico, would put a drag on one of the best currency rallies in emerging markets, according to BBVA Mexico.

item.title: Raízen Bondholders Seek $1.6 Billion Injection, Chairman Change
item.description: Raízen SA bondholders have presented the Brazilian company with a new restructuring proposal, which includes a capital injection of around 8 billion reais ($1.6 billion), according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Diesel Traders Turn to Rail as War Scrambles Fuel Flows
item.description: Demand for shipping diesel by rail within the United States surged in March, another sign of how the war in Iran has disrupted trading patterns and left the world scrambling for the vital fuel.

item.title: Defense Parts Firm Arxis Shares Jump 36% After $1.13 Billion IPO
item.description: Arxis Inc. shares surged 36% in its trading debut, after the company raised $1.13 billion in an upsized initial public offering.

item.title: Oil Producers, Shipowners in Standoff Over Who Takes Hormuz Risk
item.description: Shipowners and charterers are struggling to agree on who should take on the risk of crossing the Strait of Hormuz, meaning relatively few vessel bookings are occurring.

item.title: Retail Traders Look Past War Angst and Race Back Into Stocks
item.description: The war in Iran that triggered a record oil spike and sent financial assets around the globe careening seems like a distant memory to investors — even before it’s over.

item.title: Cómo Anthropic descubrió que Mythos era demasiado peligroso
item.description: Los propios expertos de la empresa de IA advirtieron que Mythos podría vulnerar los sistemas que sustentan gran parte de la computación moderna. Bancos y agencias gubernamentales se apresuran a evaluar la amenaza.

item.title: Alstom Scraps Finance Targets as Rail Delays Hit Profit
item.description: Alstom SA withdrew certain financial guidance for this fiscal year, with new Chief Executive Officer Martin Sion citing slow progress on key projects for railway rolling stock.

item.title: Safest Auto Loan Bonds Weaken as Borrower Delinquencies Rise
item.description: Bonds tied to US prime auto loans are weakening, signaling that investors are getting slightly more worried about the strength of consumers as more borrowers fall behind on car debt payments.

item.title: NYC Pension Plans to Invest $4 Billion in Affordable Housing
item.description: New York City’s $320 billion pension funds plan to invest $4 billion in affordable apartments, doubling their investment amid a city-wide housing crunch.

item.title: Macron Calls For United EU Front on Youth Social Media Limits
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron said Europe must come up with a cohesive approach to protecting children online as the bloc grapples with how to regulate social networks.

item.title: Mortgage Rates Drop for Second Week to 6.3%, Freddie Mac Says
item.description: Mortgage rates in the US fell for a second week as the spring home sales season gets off to a sluggish start.

item.title: SNB Backs Measures to Boost Competition on Cashless Payments
item.description: More competition in the processing of cashless payments would benefit consumers and improve resilience, according to two senior Swiss National Bank officials, who said authorities can take measures to encourage this.

item.title: Why Oil Futures Are Trading Way Below Real-World Prices
item.description: Six weeks into the Iran war, crude oil markets are experiencing one of their worst-ever disruptions, triggered by the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Hundreds of ships are bottled up in the Persian Gulf, unable to carry their cargoes out of the region.

item.title: Europe’s Auto Heartland Saw Cash Flow Nearly Halve Since 2019
item.description: Europe’s auto industry has a growing cash problem, even as earnings still look steady.

item.title: Turkey Officials Tell Investors War May Dent Growth, Lift Prices
item.description: Turkey’s top economic policymakers told investors this week that the fallout from the Iran war is likely to weigh on Turkish growth and raise price pressures in the short term, according to people with knowledge of the conversations.

item.title: Uranium Royalty to Buy Sweetwater in $1.1 Billion Nuclear Fuel Deal
item.description: Uranium Royalty Corp. agreed to buy Sweetwater Royalties for about $1.1 billion in a deal that would create a new US-listed company to capitalize on growing demand for nuclear fuel.

item.title: Private Credit Funds Face More Pressure as Banks Swap Collateral
item.description: For years, Wall Street banks eagerly helped private credit funds amplify their investing firepower with hundreds of billions of dollars in loans, helping them notch ever-higher returns.

item.title: South Africa Has Room for $732 Million Fuel Relief Extension, Citi Says
item.description: South Africa has enough fiscal space to extend a fuel-tax cut by two months to cushion consumers from an oil shock triggered by the Iran war, Citigroup Inc. said.

item.title: SEC Asks for Input on Slimming CAT Trade Data Collection, Costs
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission asked for industry input on how to trim down the cost and scope of trading data that exchanges and brokers report to a centralized database.

item.title: US Factory Output Rebound in First Quarter Extended Beyond AI
item.description: US factory output snapped back in the first quarter after disappointing at the end of 2025, and the advance extended beyond the artificial intelligence build-out.

item.title: ECB’s Schnabel Says It’s Good Time to Discuss Euro Bonds Again
item.description: European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel said now is a “good time” to again debate joint European Union borrowing.

item.title: IMF Says Egypt Has Built Buffers to Tackle Shocks as Pound Gains
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said Egypt’s economic reforms and stronger buffers are enabling the country to better weather external shocks, as the local pound reverses some losses after the Iran war ceasefire.
